task_completion.description: The sourdough image is opened in Fiji and the scale is calibrated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Fiji GUI showing the sourdough bread image. Look for the 'Set Scale' dialog or the image window header/status bar showing dimensions in 'cm' instead of 'pixels'.

task_completion.description: A central 5x5 cm Region of Interest (ROI) is created to exclude the crust.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A yellow rectangular selection box is visible in the center of the bread slice, clearly avoiding the outer crust.

task_completion.description: Dark air voids are segmented and filtered by size.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image shows a thresholding mask (red overlay or black/white binary image) isolating the dark pores. The 'Analyze Particles' dialog may be visible showing a 'Size' filter of '0.005-Infinity'.

task_completion.description: The pore measurements are saved to a CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or text editor showing the file 'pore_measurements.csv' located in '~/Fiji_Data/results/food_science/'.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the segmentation mask is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or image viewer showing 'segmentation_check.png' located in '~/Fiji_Data/results/food_science/'. The image should depict the black and white (or outlined) pore mask.

task_completion.description: A quality report text file is created containing the calculated Porosity % and Mean Pore Area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of 'quality_report.txt' in '~/Fiji_Data/results/food_science/'. The text must explicitly list 'Porosity' (or Void Fraction) as a percentage and 'Mean Pore Area' in cm².

integrity.description: The agent used the Fiji (ImageJ) graphical interface to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Fiji application window, menus, and dialogs (like Threshold or Analyze Particles) being actively used, rather than just terminal commands or Python scripts doing the work.

integrity.description: The segmentation mask corresponds to the actual bread image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The pattern of the pores in the 'segmentation_check.png' or Fiji mask window should visually match the dark voids of the original sourdough bread slice, not a generic or blank image.

integrity.description: The values in the quality report are derived from actual analysis and fall within expected scientific ranges.
integrity.visual_evidence: In 'quality_report.txt', the Porosity should be a plausible value (typically between 30% and 60%) and the Mean Pore Area should be between 0.01 and 0.5 cm². Values exactly matching the bounds or wildly outside them indicate hallucination.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Porosity is between 30.0% and 60.0%. Expected Mean Pore Area is between 0.01 and 0.5 cm². The noise threshold for filtering particles is 0.005 cm².
